name: Spring
description: Vernal equinox, cherry blossoms, March-May — the season of rebirth.

questions.q: What astronomical event marks the start of spring in the Northern Hemisphere?
When it's spring in the Northern Hemisphere, what season is it in the Southern Hemisphere?
Which months are spring in the U.S./UK by meteorological definition?

source.notes: Astronomical spring runs from equinox to solstice (~March 20 - June 21).

questions.q: On the equinox, day and night are approximately what length each?
Which Persian New Year falls on the first day of spring?

source.notes: Celebrated on the vernal equinox; UN-recognized cultural heritage.

questions.q: Which Japanese springtime tradition involves picnicking under blooming cherry trees?

source.notes: Hanami means 'flower viewing' (the activity); Sakura means 'cherry blossom' (the tree itself). audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed awkward 'Both' meta-answer; replaced with a clearly-wrong 'Origami' distractor and made Hanami the direct correct answer.

questions.q: Which spring holiday begins on the Sunday after the first full moon after the vernal equinox?
Which March 17 holiday is celebrated with shamrocks and parades?
What weather event peaks during spring in the U.S. Tornado Alley?

source.notes: Peak season: April-June for the Plains states.

questions.q: Which spring observance is known as Earth Day, celebrating environmental awareness?
Which white flower is typical of early spring, often pushing up through snow?

source.notes: Genus Galanthus. audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced 'Crocus' distractor (which is also widely cited as a snow-pushing early-spring flower) with 'Sunflower' to avoid ambiguity.

questions.q: Which spring flower is the official symbol of the Netherlands and Turkey?

source.notes: Tulips originated in Central Asia and Turkey before becoming Dutch icons.

questions.q: Which traditional U.S. observance celebrates the start of Daylight Saving Time in spring?

source.notes: Second Sunday of March in U.S.

questions.q: Which famous Washington D.C. spring festival celebrates Japan's gift of cherry trees from 1912?

source.notes: First trees: gift from Mayor of Tokyo Yukio Ozaki, 1912.

questions.q: What term describes the surge of energy/restlessness many people feel at the start of spring?
Which Hindu festival of colors falls on the full moon of the Hindu month of Phalguna (typically March)?
Which two religious holidays fall during spring (often in March-April)?
What's the famous baseball term for the warmup season in February-March?

source.notes: Held in Florida (Grapefruit League) and Arizona (Cactus League).

questions.q: Which 1816 'Year Without a Summer' had abnormally cold spring temperatures from a volcano?

source.notes: Largest volcanic eruption in recorded history. Caused snow in June across the Northern Hemisphere.

questions.q: Which Persian/Middle Eastern tradition involves jumping over fire on the last Wednesday before Nowruz?

source.notes: Festival of Fire — sym­bolic burning of bad luck.
